title: Earthquake aftershock sleep layout
summary: Reduces night injury risk after an earthquake when aftershocks are expected.
content: Earthquake aftershock sleep layout is a practical offline routine for earthquake shelter. Move beds away from windows, mirrors, tall furniture, shelves, hanging objects, and cracked walls if the building is safe. Place shoes, flashlight, glasses, medicine, and go-bag where they can be reached without stepping on glass. Keep doors and exit paths clear and set a no-elevator rule after shaking. Agree on an outside meeting point. Sleep in clothing suitable for fast exit and weather. The goal is to make the action repeatable by a tired person, visible to helpers, and safe enough to stop when official help or a safer option appears.
steps: Move beds away from windows, mirrors, tall furniture, shelves, hanging objects, and cracked walls if the building is safe.
Place shoes, flashlight, glasses, medicine, and go-bag where they can be reached without stepping on glass.
Keep doors and exit paths clear and set a no-elevator rule after shaking.
Agree on an outside meeting point.
Sleep in clothing suitable for fast exit and weather.
warnings: Use Earthquake aftershock sleep layout as practical support, not as a replacement for official guidance, trained help, or evacuation when needed.
Generators, grills, and fuel-burning devices must never operate inside homes, garages, or other enclosed occupied spaces in “Earthquake aftershock sleep layout”.
common_mistakes: Starting from memory instead of written notes while carrying out “Earthquake aftershock sleep layout”
Letting one tired person control the whole process while carrying out “Earthquake aftershock sleep layout”
Ignoring the review time while carrying out “Earthquake aftershock sleep layout”
alternatives: Use a wall board when many people are involved as a fallback for “Earthquake aftershock sleep layout”
Use pocket cards when movement is required as a fallback for “Earthquake aftershock sleep layout”
failure_signs: People disagree about the facts, the plan is not being followed, or the situation worsens after the first review during “Earthquake aftershock sleep layout”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Earthquake aftershock sleep layout to delay emergency care, evacuation, fire response, or official instructions.
short_term: Write the first safe action, responsible person, materials, and next review time. This note applies specifically to Earthquake aftershock sleep layout.
long_term: Convert the routine into a repeatable checklist with handover notes and backups. This note applies specifically to Earthquake aftershock sleep layout.
if_method_fails: Reduce complexity, stop unsafe work, and switch to the safest available fallback. This note applies specifically to Earthquake aftershock sleep layout.
environment_notes: Adapt the routine to heat, cold, crowding, darkness, limited water, and local hazards. This note applies specifically to Earthquake aftershock sleep layout.
